# Parts Consumption

> Record parts and materials consumed during vehicle service or repair events, validate stock availability, trigger inventory deductions, and attribute parts cost to the service record.

**Category:** Inventory · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** fleet · vehicle · parts · inventory · consumption · service · stock

## What this does

Record parts and materials consumed during vehicle service or repair events, validate stock availability, trigger inventory deductions, and attribute parts cost to the service record.

Specifies 6 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **service_record** *(text, required)* — Service Record Reference
- **vehicle** *(text, required)* — Vehicle
- **item_code** *(text, required)* — Item Code
- **item_description** *(text, optional)* — Item Description
- **warehouse** *(text, required)* — Source Warehouse
- **quantity** *(number, required)* — Quantity
- **unit_of_measure** *(text, required)* — Unit of Measure
- **unit_cost** *(number, optional)* — Unit Cost
- **total_cost** *(number, optional)* — Total Cost
- **batch_number** *(text, optional)* — Batch Number
- **serial_number** *(text, optional)* — Serial Number
- **stock_entry_reference** *(text, optional)* — Stock Entry Reference
- **status** *(select, required)* — Status

## What must be true

- **quantity_positive:** Quantity must be greater than zero
- **item_must_exist:** Item must exist in the inventory catalogue
- **stock_availability:** Sufficient stock must be available in the specified warehouse before issuing; insufficient stock blocks the transaction
- **total_cost_computation:** Total cost is computed as quantity × unit_cost; unit_cost is sourced from inventory valuation if not manually entered
- **stock_deduction_on_issue:** On confirmation (status → issued), a stock deduction transaction is created automatically in the inventory system
- **cancellation_requires_return:** Cancelling an issued consumption requires a return transaction to restore the stock
- **serial_tracking:** Serial-tracked items require a valid serial number that is currently in stock at the specified warehouse
- **batch_tracking:** Batch-tracked items require a valid batch number that has sufficient quantity available

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Consumption Cancelled** — when status is draft or reserved; cancellation requested by technician or fleet manager, then Consumption line is cancelled; any reservation is released back to available stock.
- **Cost Attributed To Service** — when parts_issued outcome has fired; service_record is linked, then Service record's parts cost total is updated to include this consumption.
- **Parts Issued** — when item_code exists in the inventory catalogue; Quantity is positive; sufficient stock is available in the specified warehouse; serial/batch requirements are met if item is tracked, then Parts are recorded as consumed, inventory is decremented, and cost is attributed to the service record.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Insufficient Stock Blocked** — when requested quantity exceeds available stock in the specified warehouse, then Issue is blocked; technician is notified of the shortfall and must select an alternative warehouse or raise a purchase request. *(error: `PARTS_INSUFFICIENT_STOCK`)*
- **Invalid Quantity Rejected** — when Quantity is zero or negative, then Consumption line is rejected with a quantity validation error. *(error: `PARTS_INVALID_QUANTITY`)*
- **Serial Number Invalid** — when item is serialised; serial_number is not in stock at the specified warehouse, then Issue blocked; technician must provide a valid serial number available in stock. *(error: `PARTS_INVALID_SERIAL`)*

## Errors it can return

- `PARTS_INSUFFICIENT_STOCK` — Insufficient stock available. Please check the warehouse or raise a parts request.
- `PARTS_INVALID_QUANTITY` — Quantity must be greater than zero.
- `PARTS_INVALID_SERIAL` — The specified serial number is not available in this warehouse.
- `PARTS_ITEM_NOT_FOUND` — The item code does not exist in the inventory catalogue.

## Connects to

- **vehicle-maintenance-log** *(required)* — Parts consumption is linked to a maintenance log record and contributes to service parts cost
- **scheduled-maintenance** *(optional)* — Scheduled service tasks may have standard parts lists that pre-populate consumption lines
